_doc: doc-coherence registry. Declares the single canonical owner of each fact/term so docs can POINT to it instead of restating it (CLAUDE.md §8 'pointers, not copies'). Run `node scripts/check-doc-coherence.js` to enforce. Schema: docs/doc-coherence.md.

facts.title: The Five Principles of AI-Native Engineering (philosophy / why)
markers: A smaller, correct implementation is always preferred
Every coding session must record reflections
facts.note: The principle NAMES (Intent First, Verify Don't Generate, ...) legitimately appear in CLAUDE.md §5 as the operational 'Five Directives'. These markers are README-exact DEFINITION sentences that must live in one place only. If a definition sentence appears outside README, two docs are defining the philosophy independently — that is the drift to catch.

facts.title: Phantom-completion check definition (Gate 2)
markers: A task marked `[x]` with no corresponding commit diff is a phantom completion
facts.note: Gate semantics are owned by VERIFICATION_AND_EVAL_GUIDE.md. governance.md and CLAUDE.md §4 summarise the gates but must not redefine this specific check.

facts.title: Glossary of canonical terms
markers: these are the canonical names. Use them exactly
facts.note: The glossary is the human-readable twin of this registry. It must exist in exactly one place; other docs link to it.
